title: salix × ambigua var. microphylla
intro: Salix × ambigua var. microphylla is a hybrid willow plant that is a cross between the Salix × sepulcralis and Salix × dasyclados, characterized by its small leaves and slender branches. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × ambigua var. microphylla include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used externally to soothe skin irritations and wounds, or taken internally to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
